machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (links the IM platform to the secretin)
machinery_genes.quote: Characterization of the first two genes of this operon showed that they encode, respectively, a 31,000-Da protein (PulC) and a 70,600-Da protein (PulD)... these two proteins are required for pullulanase secretion... This, together with sequencing data, suggests that PulC is an inner membrane protein.
machinery_genes.note: d'Enfert, Reyss, Wandersman & Pugsley 1989 (J Biol Chem). First two genes of the pul secretion operon; PulC = inner-membrane GspC-family platform protein. DOI is the Elsevier-hosted JBC PII (10.1016/S0021-9258(18)71517-2); JBC vol 264:17462-8.

machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin (the OM channel through which pullulanase exits)
machinery_genes.quote: a 70,600-Da protein (PulD) which has a putative signal peptide... Antibodies raised against a purified PulD-PhoA hybrid protein were used to show that PulD was enriched in low density outer membrane vesicles.
machinery_genes.note: d'Enfert et al 1989. PulD localizes to the outer membrane; later established as the multimeric secretin of the Pul T2SS (PulS/pilotin-dependent). GspD family per apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.role: cytoplasmic secretion ATPase (nucleotide-binding traffic ATPase that energizes the apparatus)
machinery_genes.quote: The previously uncharacterized third and fourth genes (pulE and pulF) of the pullulanase secretion gene operon of Klebsiella oxytoca strain UNF5023 are, respectively, predicted to encode a 55 kDa polypeptide with a putative nucleotide-binding site, and a highly hydrophobic 44 kDa polypeptide that probably spans the cytoplasmic membrane several times... both pulE and pulF are required for pullulanase secretion
machinery_genes.note: Possot, d'Enfert, Reyss & Pugsley 1992. PulE has the GspE-family nucleotide-binding (ATPase) motif and is cytoplasmic; pulE/pulF are the 3rd/4th genes of the pulC-O operon.

machinery_genes.role: integral inner-membrane platform component (polytopic IM protein)
machinery_genes.quote: a highly hydrophobic 44 kDa polypeptide that probably spans the cytoplasmic membrane several times... both pulE and pulF are required for pullulanase secretion in Escherichia coli K-12
machinery_genes.note: Possot et al 1992. PulF = polytopic IM platform protein, GspF family. Paper concludes the operon comprises 13 genes (pulC-O) all essential for transport of pullulanase across the outer membrane.

machinery_genes.quote: The characterized region of the operon includes five genes (pulG, pulH, pulI, pulJ and pulK) coding for apparently integral inner membrane proteins which are required for pullulanase secretion.
machinery_genes.note: Reyss & Pugsley 1990 (Mol Gen Genet) name pulG-pulK as required secretion genes in the pulC-O operon. PulG is the major pseudopilin: see Nivaskumar 2016 (PMID 27260845): 'PulG, the major pseudopilin of the Klebsiella T2SS'.

machinery_genes.quote: The characterized region of the operon includes five genes (pulG, pulH, pulI, pulJ and pulK) coding for apparently integral inner membrane proteins which are required for pullulanase secretion.
machinery_genes.note: Reyss & Pugsley 1990. Minor-pseudopilin role from Nivaskumar 2016 (PMID 27260845), which describes 'the four minor pseudopilins' and the full-length pseudopilins PulG and PulH interacting with the PulJ-PulI-PulK priming complex.

machinery_genes.role: minor pseudopilin (part of the PulI-PulJ-PulK priming/tip complex)
machinery_genes.quote: The characterized region of the operon includes five genes (pulG, pulH, pulI, pulJ and pulK) coding for apparently integral inner membrane proteins which are required for pullulanase secretion.
machinery_genes.note: Reyss & Pugsley 1990. Minor pseudopilin; Nivaskumar 2016 (PMID 27260845) names 'the PulJ-PulI-PulK priming complex'.

machinery_genes.role: minor pseudopilin (part of the PulI-PulJ-PulK priming/tip complex)
machinery_genes.quote: The characterized region of the operon includes five genes (pulG, pulH, pulI, pulJ and pulK) coding for apparently integral inner membrane proteins which are required for pullulanase secretion.
machinery_genes.note: Reyss & Pugsley 1990. Minor pseudopilin; priming-complex role from Nivaskumar 2016 (PMID 27260845).

machinery_genes.role: minor pseudopilin (part of the PulI-PulJ-PulK priming/tip complex)
machinery_genes.quote: The characterized region of the operon includes five genes (pulG, pulH, pulI, pulJ and pulK) coding for apparently integral inner membrane proteins which are required for pullulanase secretion.
machinery_genes.note: Reyss & Pugsley 1990. Minor pseudopilin; priming-complex role from Nivaskumar 2016 (PMID 27260845). pulK is the gene that bridges the two 1990 sequencing papers (3' end of Reyss & Pugsley, 5' end of Pugsley & Reyss).

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (binds/recruits the PulE ATPase)
machinery_genes.quote: The sequence includes the last four genes of an operon of genes specifically required for the secretion of the enzyme pullulanase. All four genes (pulL, pulM, pulN and pulO) are shown to be required for pullulanase secretion... The products of the pulL, pulM, pulN and pulO genes (44 kD, 18 kD, 27 kD and 24 kD, respectively)... were all found mainly associated with the inner membranes
machinery_genes.note: Pugsley & Reyss 1990 (Mol Microbiol). pulL = the GspL-family IM platform component (in V. cholerae the GspL/EpsL homolog binds the secretion ATPase). Inner-membrane localization confirmed by subcellular fractionation.

machinery_genes.quote: All four genes (pulL, pulM, pulN and pulO) are shown to be required for pullulanase secretion... The products of the pulL, pulM, pulN and pulO genes (44 kD, 18 kD, 27 kD and 24 kD, respectively)... were all found mainly associated with the inner membranes of subfractionated cells
machinery_genes.note: Pugsley & Reyss 1990. PulM = GspM-family IM platform protein; Nivaskumar 2016 (PMID 27260845) identifies PulM as a pseudopilin-recruiting assembly factor ('interaction between native PulM and PulG').

machinery_genes.quote: All four genes (pulL, pulM, pulN and pulO) are shown to be required for pullulanase secretion... The products of the pulL, pulM, pulN and pulO genes (44 kD, 18 kD, 27 kD and 24 kD, respectively)... were all found mainly associated with the inner membranes
machinery_genes.note: Pugsley & Reyss 1990. pulN = terminal accessory GspN-family gene of the operon; required for secretion and inner-membrane associated. Lower-confidence functional assignment (GspN is accessory and not present in all T2SS), but its requirement and IM localization are directly demonstrated here.

machinery_genes.role: prepilin/prepseudopilin peptidase (matures the pseudopilins, e.g. processes prePulG)
machinery_genes.quote: Processing of prePulG in Escherichia coli requires a glycine at position -1, as does processing by the cognate prepilin peptidase (PulO)
machinery_genes.note: PulO is the last gene of the pulC-O operon (Pugsley & Reyss 1990, PMID 2162463, names pulO among the 'last four genes... required for pullulanase secretion'). Its enzymatic identity as the cognate prepilin/pseudopilin peptidase that matures PulG is established by Francetic, Lory & Pugsley 1998 (this quote). GspO family.

notes: The Pul system is the archetypal T2SS: the Klebsiella oxytoca (originally classified K. pneumoniae) pullulanase secretion machinery characterized gene-by-gene by Pugsley and colleagues at Institut Pasteur, and the system after which the Gsp C-O nomenclature is patterned. The apparatus is the pulC-O operon (13 genes), all directly demonstrated to be required for pullulanase secretion and all named in primary sequencing/mutagenesis papers: pulC,D (d'Enfert 1989, PMID 2677007); pulE,F (Possot 1992, PMID 1738317, which concludes 'the operon... comprises 13 genes that are all essential for the transport of pullulanase across the outer membrane'); pulG,H,I,J,K (Reyss & Pugsley 1990, PMID 2129543); pulL,M,N,O (Pugsley & Reyss 1990, PMID 2162463). Pseudopilin and assembly-factor roles (PulG major pseudopilin; PulH,I,J,K minor pseudopilins; PulM recruiting factor) are assigned from Nivaskumar 2016 (PMID 27260845). PulO's prepilin-peptidase identity (maturing PulG) is from Francetic 1998 (PMID 9515702). Original strain in the apparatus papers is K. (pneumoniae/oxytoca) UNF5023; this instance is M5al (= the same Pul system family, the standard pullulanase-secreting K. oxytoca lab strain) — note as strain transfer within the same system. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: pulA (pullulanase, the secreted substrate/effector, not machinery); pulS (the OM-lipoprotein pilotin/chaperone for the PulD secretin, PMID 2661532) — it is a genuine accessory component but lies OUTSIDE the labeled pulC-O operon (encoded downstream of pulA, transcribed opposite to pulA, malT-independent) and functions as a secretin-targeting chaperone rather than a translocation channel/platform subunit, so it is left out to match the pulC-O scope; the Sec/Tat general export translocon (general IM transport, not T2SS-specific); and the malT/maltose regulatory genes (transcriptional regulators). Lowest-confidence call: PulN (terminal GspN accessory gene; requirement and IM localization demonstrated, but functional role least defined). All quotes verbatim from the cited PubMed abstracts/records; DOIs and PMIDs verified via get_article_metadata.
